name: AI Security Guard
description: Real-time AI agent input/output security scanning platform. Detects and blocks prompt injection, data exfiltration, jailbreaking, and adversarial inputs before they reach LLMs. Sits as middleware between users/agents and the AI model, acting as a firewall for agentic systems.
oneLiner: Real-time AI agent security scanning with prompt injection detection, data leak prevention, and policy enforcement.
pricing: Usage-based per scan. Free tier for evaluation. Enterprise custom pricing.
capabilities: SDK for LLM providers and agent frameworks

uxAnalysis.builderFlow: Not agent builder. Integrate SDK, configure policies via web console: select threat categories, set thresholds, define custom patterns.
strengths: Addresses critical gap in enterprise agent security
weaknesses: False positive/negative rates are industry challenge
notableFeatures: Prompt injection detection via specialized ML
Multi-turn jailbreak detection across conversation context

recentDevelopments: SDK integrations for LangChain, CrewAI, OpenAI/Anthropic
Growing partnerships with agent platform vendors

subCriteria.name: Agent Builder UX
subCriteria.rationale: Policy config via web console. Not agent builder.

subCriteria.name: Time-to-Value
subCriteria.rationale: Policy testing with samples. Scan result inspector.

subCriteria.name: Debugging & Testing
subCriteria.rationale: API docs and integration guides. Early-stage depth.

subCriteria.name: Documentation & Learning
subCriteria.rationale: Policy changes immediate. No staged rollout.

subCriteria.name: Multi-Agent Coordination
subCriteria.rationale: Not orchestration. Security scanning service.

subCriteria.name: Human-in-the-Loop Design
subCriteria.rationale: Flagged threat review as basic human interaction.

subCriteria.name: Workflow Templates
subCriteria.rationale: Single-team policy management.

subCriteria.name: Team Development
subCriteria.rationale: Some pre-built policy templates.

subCriteria.name: Native Connectors
subCriteria.rationale: SDKs for OpenAI, Anthropic, LangChain, CrewAI. Webhooks.

subCriteria.name: API & Protocol Standards
subCriteria.rationale: REST API. Python/JS SDKs. Early MCP. No gRPC.

subCriteria.name: Authentication & Access
subCriteria.rationale: API keys. SSO on enterprise. Basic.

subCriteria.name: Data Pipeline & RAG
subCriteria.rationale: No data pipeline. Operates on individual inputs.

subCriteria.name: Guardrails & Kill Switches
subCriteria.rationale: THIS IS the guardrails product. Real-time blocking. Policy enforcement.

subCriteria.name: Audit Trail & Compliance
subCriteria.rationale: Flagged inputs routed for review. Basic quarantine.

subCriteria.name: RBAC & Access Control
subCriteria.rationale: Scan logs comprehensive. Compliance certs in progress.

subCriteria.name: Explainability & Transparency
subCriteria.rationale: Basic admin/viewer roles. Team management in enterprise.

subCriteria.name: Deployment Flexibility
subCriteria.rationale: Cloud scanning service. SDK for inline. On-prem may be available.

subCriteria.name: Scaling & Monitoring
subCriteria.rationale: High-throughput, low-latency by design. Auto-scaling.

subCriteria.name: Cost Management
subCriteria.rationale: Threat dashboards, scan metrics, policy match rates.

subCriteria.name: Ecosystem Maturity
subCriteria.rationale: Early-stage. Small customer base. Growing AI security visibility.

scoringRationale: AI Security Guard scores 55/100. Governance (14) is standout — guardrails ARE the product. Builder (12) and Deployment (12) decent for focused tool. Collaboration (5) and Ecosystem (1) minimal — point solution, not platform.
targetUser: Security teams protecting AI agent deployments from attacks

walkthrough.title: How AI Security Guard Works: Real-Time AI Agent Firewall

steps.title: Integrate the SDK
steps.description: Add the AI Security Guard SDK to your agent framework or LLM pipeline. It sits as middleware between users/agents and the AI model — all inputs and outputs pass through the scanning layer with sub-100ms latency.

steps.title: Configure Threat Policies
steps.description: Use the web console to select which threat categories to monitor: prompt injection, PII/data leakage, jailbreak attempts, toxicity, and custom patterns. Set sensitivity thresholds per category and define block vs. alert behavior.

steps.title: Real-Time Input Scanning
steps.description: Every prompt and agent input is scanned in real-time before reaching the LLM. Multi-turn jailbreak detection catches sophisticated attacks that span multiple messages. Malicious inputs are blocked and logged.

steps.title: Output Monitoring and DLP
steps.description: Agent outputs are scanned for sensitive data leakage (PII, credentials, internal data), policy violations, and harmful content. Sensitive data is redacted or blocked before reaching the end user.

steps.title: Audit and Incident Response
steps.description: All scans are logged with full context for audit and compliance. Security dashboards show threat trends, blocked attempts, and policy effectiveness. Alerts integrate with SIEM and incident response workflows.
